headline: OpenAI's Robotics Division Loses Key Leader Caitlin Kalinowski Over Disagreement on Military Deployment Terms
summary: On Saturday, Caitlin Kalinowski said she resigned from OpenAI, arguing that potential uses of AI for warrantless monitoring of Americans and weapon systems operating without a human decision demanded more careful debate than they received.

headline: 'If You're Gen-Z and Can Hit Your Mandate, Deliver on Time, You Move up and You Make More Money,' Says Kevin O'Leary
summary: On Saturday, Kevin O'Leary argued that the pandemic rewired how employers judge performance, shifting from clock-watching to a deliverables-first model where promotions and pay follow on-time execution.

headline: Bristol Myers Wins FDA Approval for Sotyktu for Psoriatic Arthritis
summary: Bristol Myers won FDA approval for Sotyktu in active psoriatic arthritis, expanding its oral immunology franchise despite a slight after-hours stock dip.

headline: $30K Boost in Buying Power Reshapes Home Market for Aspiring Buyers
summary: According to a report by, Zillow Group Inc. (NASDAQ: Z), a median-income U.S. household can now swing a home priced at $331,483, a jump of $30,302 from a year earlier and the strongest affordability reading since March 2022.

headline: Elon Musk Endorses 'Fundable' Jurassic Park: These De-Extinction Companies Are Already Making It Happen
summary: Elon Musk tweeted about wanting Jurassic Park in real life. Companies aren't bringing back dinosaurs yet, but they are bringing extinct items back to life.

headline: Scaramucci Says Russia May Further Stoke US-Iran Conflict
summary: On Saturday, Anthony Scaramucci accused Russia of helping Iran target American troops and said the Trump administration moved to loosen restrictions on Russian oil activity on the same day.

headline: Hegseth Brushes Off Russian Assistance to Iran: 'We're Not Concerned About That'
summary: On Saturday, Pete Hegseth said the Trump administration is watching reports that Russia is feeding Iran information about U.S. military positioning in the Middle East, arguing the U.S. can counter any such help as fighting intensifies.

headline: Trump: Iran No Longer 'Bully of the Middle East,' Now 'Loser of the Middle East'
summary: On Saturday, President Trump said Iran had issued an apology to neighboring Middle East countries and agreed to stop firing at them, framing the move as a capitulation driven by sustained U.S. and Israeli military pressure.

headline: New White House Cyber Strategy Boosts Crypto and Blockchain
summary: President Trump's updated cyber strategy gave crypto and blockchain a policy lift by naming them part of America's emerging technology priorities.

headline: Engineer Describes 19-Hour Workday at Elon Musk's xAI in Viral Post: 'I've Never Felt More Alive'
summary: xAI engineer Giri Kuncoro's viral posts spotlight the startup's punishing work culture, echoing Elon Musk's long-held belief in extreme hours and relentless intensity.

headline: Mark Zuckerberg Visited A Temple In India On Steve Jobs' Advice — Here's What It Taught Him About People And Business
summary: Mark Zuckerberg said advice from Steve Jobs to visit a temple in India during Facebook's early struggles helped him refocus on the company's mission and ultimately decide against selling it despite major buyout offers.

headline: Bill Gates Once Revealed A Vital Personality Trait That Breeds Success But Also Admitted It Doesn't Come Easy
summary: Bill Gates emphasizes the importance of building confidence in youth, as it provides the courage to overcome obstacles and discover one's true path. Supportive adults and relationships help one take risks and find their passion, as demonstrated by Gates' own experience and other successful business leaders. Fake it till you make it and trusting your instincts are also key aspects of building self-confidence.

headline: Marc Benioff Said Mass AI Layoffs Weren't Coming. Then Came A Brutal Week For White-Collar Jobs.
summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff downplayed fears of AI causing mass layoffs, but Morgan Stanley, Oracle, Capital One made headlines on job cuts during the same week. A comment that didn't age well?

headline: ORCL, BULL, SNDK And More: 5 Stocks Investors Couldn't Stop Buzzing About This Week
summary: Retail investors talked up five hot stocks this week (March 2 to March 6) on X and Reddit's r/WallStreetBets: ORCL, BULL, SNDK, PLTR, AVGO.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Costco, Plug Power, Berkshire — And Oil Prices Rise As Iran War Continues
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors' favorite stocks over the last week — here's a look at some of our top stories.

headline: Wendy's Offers 'A Job AI Can't Steal'— Could You Be Their $100K Chief Tasting Officer?
summary: Fast-food chain Wendy's (NASDAQ: WEN) has launched a nationwide contest, promising a $100,000 package for the role of "Chief Tasting Officer". The contest, which started on March 2, will continue until March 30.

headline: What To Know About Google CEO Sundar Pichai's New $692 Million Equity Pay Package
summary: Alphabet ties CEO Sundar Pichai's new equity package to the performance of Waymo and Wing, reflecting confidence in the growth potential of its "Other Bets."

headline: Lockheed Martin Goes Full Throttle On Weapons Production— Commits To 4X Output After Trump Meeting
summary: Lockheed Martin said it will quadruple munitions production following talks with Donald Trump and other defense leaders, citing the need to expand U.S. military capacity.

headline: Peter Schiff Warns Iran War Could Cost US $1 Trillion And Send Inflation 'Skyrocketing' As Mohamed El-Erian Flags Risk Of Supply Chain 'Sudden Stops'
summary: Economist Peter Schiff warned that a prolonged Iran war could cost the U.S. up to $1 trillion and drive inflation sharply higher, while Mohamed El-Erian cautioned that escalating conflict disrupting oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz could trigger supply chain shocks and broader economic risks.

headline: Elizabeth Warren Slams Trump For Rising Gas Prices As Strait Of Hormuz Crisis Threatens Global Oil Supply: 'Broken His Promises'
summary: Elizabeth Warren criticized Donald Trump over rising gas prices tied to the Iran conflict, as tensions around the Strait of Hormuz threaten global oil supplies and push crude prices higher.

headline: These Analysts Revise Their Forecasts On Samsara Following Upbeat Q4 Earnings
summary: Samsara Inc (NYSE:IOT) reported higher-than-expected Q4 earnings and raised guidance. Analysts have positive outlook, with price targets up.

headline: Pentagon Tech Chief Emil Michael Slams Anthropic As AI Weapons And Drone Autonomy Fight Escalates: 'Need Someone Who's Not Going To Wig Out'
summary: Pentagon tech chief Emil Michael criticized Anthropic for restricting its AI from use in autonomous weapons and surveillance, escalating a dispute after the Pentagon labeled the company a supply chain risk while the firm prepares a legal challenge.

headline: Trump Demands Iran's 'Unconditional Surrender' As Tehran Pushes Back On Say In Next Supreme Leader— 'Cannot Even Appoint...'
summary: President Trump demanded Iran's "unconditional surrender" and a say in selecting its next leader, a move Tehran swiftly rejected, insisting only Iranians will decide their future.

headline: Controversial FDA Vaccines Chief Vinay Prasad To Leave Agency In April
summary: Dr. Vinay Prasad is set to leave the FDA after a turbulent tenure marked by controversial decisions and a push for stricter vaccine approval standards.

headline: Chuck Schumer Warns Trump Is 'Losing Control' After Report Says Russia Is Giving Iran Intel On US Forces
summary: Chuck Schumer warned that the Middle East conflict could be expanding after a The Washington Post report said Russia is sharing intelligence with Iran on U.S. military assets, as Washington considers temporarily easing some Russian oil restrictions to stabilize global markets.

headline: Novo Nordisk And Hims & Hers End Bitter Legal Feud With Surprise Weight-Loss Drug Deal: Report
summary: Novo Nordisk and Hims & Hers Health have reportedly ended their legal feud and are preparing a new partnership to sell Novo's weight-loss drugs on the telehealth platform, surprising investors and sending Hims' stock sharply higher.

headline: Kuwait Reportedly Cuts Oil Output As Storage Fills Amid Strait of Hormuz Disruptions
summary: Kuwait has begun cutting oil production as storage reaches capacity amid Middle East tensions disrupting the Strait of Hormuz.
